trickyParts: Removing load() introduces a theoretical startup race window where Match() returns empty until watch() populates the cache. Analysis shows this is safe: Get() has a KV fallback for agent-targeted messages, the consumer uses DeliverNew() so no historical messages are at risk, and WatchAll() initial delivery is faster than the old N sequential Gets approach.
deviations: Removed the entire load() function rather than just skipping the call — it was dead code after removing the call site, so clean removal is better than leaving it
